message:
fix(time,net,server,service): use time-getter-aware Sleep to prevent wall-clock contamination in custom-clock tests

Four bugs where Sleep/TimeoutFuture created with Sleep::new() used wall clock
in their Future::poll path, defeating injected custom time getters:

- happy_eyeballs: sleep_until_with_time_getter used dual poll (custom + wall)
- tcp::stream: future_with_timeout used TimeoutFuture::new instead of with_time_getter
- server::shutdown: wait_until created Sleep::new then polled with wall clock
- service::buffer: close_race test spin-wait too short (1000 → 10000 with sleeps)

Fix: use Sleep::with_time_getter / TimeoutFuture::with_time_getter so the
custom time source is used consistently in both poll_with_time and poll paths.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
